title: Interpret The Disconnected AGV Count On The Map Monitor
summary: Use the map monitor disconnected AGV count to understand how many AGVs the RMS considers disconnected, and compare that count against source-described causes such as Wi-Fi communication loss or an AGV being turned off.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing the map monitor summary area to understand the meaning of the disconnected AGV count and to compare the displayed count against source-described disconnect causes.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to prove the exact root cause of a disconnect when the source only provides possible reasons.
Do not use logs alone to conclude that an AGV was turned off; the source states logs may show disconnecting but may not show whether the AGV was turned off.
Do not use this runbook as a recovery procedure to restore AGV communication.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the map monitor disconnected AGV count, interpret its meaning using the source definition, compare it to visible site observations, and document the count and any directly supported possible cause.

safety_notes: This is a reference and interpretation procedure only; it does not direct physical intervention.
Do not over-interpret the disconnected count or logs beyond what is directly supported by the source and site observation.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the map monitor summary area
Visibility into AGV operating state or site observation

access_required: Access to the map monitor summary area
system_preconditions: The map monitor summary area is visible to the user
The disconnected AGV count is displayed in the summary area

visual_references.description: Map monitor summary area training frame showing the disconnected AGV count among other summary indicators.

steps.title: Locate the disconnected AGV count
steps.purpose: Identify the map monitor summary field that reports disconnected AGVs.
steps.instruction: Open or view the map monitor summary area and locate the disconnected AGV count indicator.
steps.expected_result: The disconnected AGV count field is visible in the map monitor summary area.
steps.healthy_condition: The disconnected AGV count indicator can be clearly identified in the summary area.
steps.failure_condition: The disconnected AGV count cannot be located or the summary area is not visible.

actions.label: Find disconnected AGV count
actions.exact_action: Locate the disconnected AGV count in the map monitor summary area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The map monitor summary area showing the disconnected AGV count among the summary indicators.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the disconnected AGV count cannot be located in the visible map monitor summary area.

source_evidence.text: Locate the disconnected AGV count in the map monitor summary area.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The next one is the count of AGVs that are disconnected.
The artifact covers the map monitor summary area and explains ... count of disconnected AGVs.

steps.title: Read the displayed disconnected count
steps.purpose: Capture the number of AGVs currently shown as disconnected by RMS.
steps.instruction: Read the displayed disconnected AGV count from the map monitor summary area.
steps.expected_result: A numeric disconnected AGV count is identified from the display.
steps.healthy_condition: The count is visible and readable.
steps.failure_condition: The count is not readable, not present, or cannot be confirmed from the display.

actions.label: Read disconnected count
actions.exact_action: Read the displayed count of AGVs that the RMS considers disconnected.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The disconnected AGV count value in the map monitor summary area.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the displayed count cannot be read or confirmed.

source_evidence.text: Read the displayed count of AGVs that the RMS considers disconnected.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The next one is the count of AGVs that are disconnected.

steps.title: Interpret what the count means
steps.purpose: Apply the source definition so the count is understood correctly.
steps.instruction: Interpret the displayed count as the number of AGVs that have lost communication from RMS. Use the source explanation that RMS is the application running on the server and that AGVs communicate over Wi-Fi.
steps.expected_result: The disconnected count is understood as a communication-state indicator from RMS, not just a generic AGV status number.
steps.healthy_condition: The count is interpreted as AGVs considered disconnected because communication from RMS has been lost.
steps.failure_condition: The count is interpreted as something other than the source-defined RMS communication disconnect state.

actions.label: Apply source definition
actions.exact_action: Interpret the count as AGVs that have lost communication from RMS, using the source definition that RMS is the server application and AGVs communicate over Wi-Fi.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the displayed count cannot be reconciled with the source definition of disconnected AGVs.

source_evidence.text: Interpret the count as AGVs that have lost communication from RMS, using the source definition that RMS is the server application and AGVs communicate over Wi-Fi.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: AGVs have lost communication from RMS... RMS is the application running on the server... The AGVs are on the Wi-Fi.
RMS, the robot management system, described as the brains behind the AGVs ... users access the RMS web application.

steps.title: Compare against source-described possible causes
steps.purpose: Check whether the observed disconnected condition matches causes explicitly described in the source.
steps.instruction: Check whether the disconnected condition could match source-described causes such as AGV Wi-Fi communication loss or an AGV being turned off. Treat these as possible causes only unless directly supported by observation.
steps.expected_result: Any observed possible cause is limited to source-supported explanations and is not overstated.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed conditions are consistent with source-described possibilities such as communication loss or a turned-off AGV.
steps.failure_condition: No visible site observation supports the possible cause, or the count cannot be reconciled with visible AGV state.

actions.label: Check possible causes
actions.exact_action: Check whether the disconnected condition could match source-described causes such as AGV Wi-Fi communication loss or an AGV being turned off.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the disconnected count cannot be reconciled with visible AGV state or known site observations.
Stop interpretation if the only available evidence is logs that show disconnecting but do not show whether the AGV was turned off.

source_evidence.text: Check whether the disconnected condition could match source-described causes such as AGV Wi-Fi communication loss or an AGV being turned off.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: It can happen for multiple reasons... or it was turned off.
Disconnects can result from communication loss or an AGV being turned off.

steps.title: Record the count and directly supported observation
steps.purpose: Document the disconnected count and any possible cause only when supported by direct observation.
steps.instruction: Record the disconnected AGV count and the observed possible cause if it is directly supported by site observation. Do not record an unsupported root-cause conclusion.
steps.expected_result: The disconnected count is documented with only source-supported and observation-supported interpretation.
steps.healthy_condition: Documentation includes the count and any directly observed possible cause without overstatement.
steps.failure_condition: Documentation assigns an unsupported cause or omits the observed count.

actions.label: Document count and observation
actions.exact_action: Record the disconnected count and the observed possible cause if it is directly supported by site observation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the disconnected count cannot be reconciled with visible AGV state or known site observations.
Do not finalize a cause statement if the source limitation about logs prevents confirmation.

source_evidence.text: Record the disconnected count and the observed possible cause if it is directly supported by site observation.
The source states that logs may show disconnecting but may not show whether the AGV was turned off, so do not over-interpret logs.

success_criteria: The disconnected AGV count is located and read from the map monitor summary area.
The count is interpreted as the number of AGVs that RMS considers disconnected due to lost communication.
Any possible cause noted is limited to source-described possibilities and direct site observation.
The count and any directly supported observation are documented without over-interpreting logs.
healthy_conditions: The disconnected AGV count is visible in the map monitor summary area.
The user understands that the count represents AGVs that have lost communication from RMS.
Observed conditions align with source-described possibilities such as Wi-Fi communication loss or an AGV being turned off.
failure_conditions: The disconnected AGV count cannot be located or read.
The count cannot be reconciled with visible AGV state or known site observations.
Logs are used to infer a turned-off AGV when the source does not support that conclusion.
An unsupported root cause is assigned.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the disconnected count cannot be reconciled with visible AGV state or known site observations.
Escalate when the available evidence is insufficient to distinguish between communication loss and a powered-off AGV.
Do not over-interpret logs; if logs only show disconnecting, document that limitation and escalate as needed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Map monitor summary area showing the disconnected AGV count.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The next one is the count of AGVs that are disconnected... AGVs have lost communication from RMS... RMS is the application running on the server... The AGVs are on the Wi-Fi... it can happen for multiple reasons... or it was turned off.

missing_details: The source does not provide a numeric threshold for when the disconnected AGV count is abnormal.
The source does not provide a recovery action to restore communication.
The source does not define a required documentation system or record format.
The source does not specify exact escalation destination or timing.
The source does not provide a time estimate for this reference procedure.
